title: allamanda puberula
intro: Allamanda puberula, also known as the hairy allamanda, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Allamanda puberula include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and crushing the leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or used in topical applications.
